content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Thrill Labs: DevOps Engineer Anywhere in the World Full-Stack Programming Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote from EU </p> <p><strong>About the company</strong></p><p>At Thrill Labs, we're not just building a gaming company; we're creating a global sensation. We are the innovative force behind <a href="http://Thrill.com">Thrill.com</a>, a groundbreaking crypto gaming venture set to launch soon! Our mission? To build the world’s most epic gaming brand and craft magical experiences for millions of users. Backed by a world-class team, we’re here to redefine the future of gaming. </p><p></p><p><strong>About the role<br></strong>We’re a fast-growing tech startup looking for an experienced Infrastructure/DevOps Engineer.<br>You will own our AWS cloud environment, designing secure, scalable, and cost-efficient infrastructure, managing and improving our Kubernetes clusters, networking, observability, and autoscaling.</p><p><br>We are already operating at scale, processing 10M+ transactions per day, so experience with managing high-scalability projects is required.<br>You will operate using Infrastructure-as-Code and configuration management best practices, and collaborate closely with backend/frontend teams to shape the architecture and the developer experience.</p><p><br>If you enjoy ownership, modern stacks, and solving complex problems, we’d love to hear from you.</p><p><strong><br>Core responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li><p>Build and maintain production infrastructure in AWS.</p></li><li><p>Manage Linux servers.</p></li><li><p>Operate Kubernetes clusters.</p></li><li><p>Administer and optimize PostgreSQL databases.</p></li><li><p>Operate monitoring &amp; observability.</p></li><li><p>Be part of the on-call rotation for the infrastructure components.</p></li><li><p>Ownership of the CI/CD process.</p></li><li><p>Work on improving infrastructure and application security.</p></li><li><p>Manage CloudFlare, WAF, and DDoS protection solutions to improve our stance in this area.</p></li></ul><p><strong>Requirements</strong></p><ul><li><p>7–10 years of Infra/DevOps experience (minimum 5 years).</p></li><li><p>Hands‑on AWS, Kubernetes, Postgres, Linux.</p></li><li><p>Strong networking + monitoring/observability experience.</p></li><li><p>Python skills and Pulumi knowledge are a plus.</p></li><li><p>Understanding or interest in infrastructure &amp; application security and threat mitigation.</p></li><li><p>English B2.</p></li></ul> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/thrill-labs-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/thrill-labs-devops-engineer</a></p> Fri, 12 Jun 2026 20:30:59 +0000 Sun, 12 Jul 2026 20:30:59 +0000 https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/thrill-labs-devops-engineer https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/thrill-labs-devops-engineer Vvd: Product Engineer Anywhere in the World Full-Stack Programming Full-Time <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Canada </p> <p>We know applying for jobs is exhausting. Endless text full of buzzwords and requirements you'll never actually use. We'll skip that.</p><p>We're <strong>vvd</strong>. We make tools for storytellers — worldbuilders, writers, creators. Our goal is simple: empower people to create worlds, stories, and experiences that others can get lost in.</p><p>We're still at the beginning of our own story, and we're looking for a few people to help write the next chapters.</p><p>In a moment where "AI creative tools" are pumping out slop on one side and vicious debate on the other, we're choosing a different path: use this technology to empower human creativity and craft, not replace it.</p><p>This isn't going to be easy — but you'll actually shape something real. Something that will reach millions of people. You'll own it end-to-end, in a way that leaves a mark you can point to and say: <em>I built that.</em></p><h3>What a Product Engineer means at vvd</h3><p>A Product Engineer here is a <strong>more technical Design Engineer</strong>.</p><p>You still care deeply about taste, feel, motion, and craft. But you also go further down the stack: data models, APIs, performance, reliability, and the messy systems work needed to ship great product.</p><p>You are also <strong>extremely comfortable with AI tooling</strong>. You use it daily (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.) to explore solutions, generate and refine code, debug faster, and ship at a pace that would be hard to match otherwise.</p><h3>What you’ll do</h3><ul><li>Build features end-to-end: product thinking → implementation → launch → iteration.</li><li>Partner tightly with design and engineering to turn ambiguous ideas into shipped product in days, not weeks.</li><li>Own UI craft <em>and</em> the underlying technical decisions that make experiences fast, reliable, and scalable.</li><li>Shape how we build: patterns, primitives, component quality, testing, and internal tooling.</li><li>Use AI tools as a multiplier. You prompt well, iterate quickly, and maintain a high quality bar.</li><li>Communicate directly and clearly. No corporate padding.</li></ul> <h3>What we care about</h3><p>Experience helps, but it’s not a gate. If you’ve built and shipped real things — jobs, side projects, experiments — and you can walk us through what you built, why you built it that way, what broke, and how you solved it, you’re good.</p><p>Ownership matters more than pedigree.</p><h3>Specifically</h3><ul><li>Strong product engineering fundamentals (TypeScript, React, Next.js or similar).</li><li>Enough backend understanding to build features without getting blocked (APIs, auth, data flow, persistence, basic infra concepts).</li><li>Taste. You can feel what’s working and what isn’t — and you can fix it.</li><li>You can go from a rough idea to a polished implementation without waiting for a perfect spec.</li><li>You think in systems: performance, edge cases, instrumentation, maintainability.</li><li>You use AI tools daily to accelerate your work — not as a crutch, but as leverage.</li></ul><h3>Extra pluses</h3><ul><li>Experience with Figma or similar design tools for rapid prototyping.</li><li>You care about accessibility and inclusive UX.</li><li>You’ve built interactions and motion that feel <em>good</em>, not just functional.</li><li>You read fiction, build worlds, or create things for fun.</li></ul> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/vvd-product-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/vvd-product-engineer</a></p> Fri, 12 Jun 2026 20:30:59 +0000 Sun, 12 Jul 2026 20:30:59 +0000 https://weworkremotely.com/remo
